comment.body:
@Kidd_Rio Let me react to your comment here as well, bcs it is useful for some members. 
You have to accept two things: 1) these are my analysis, and I will interpret my thought process as it is, this is why plp asked me to make this service (and everything I talk about is far from math), if it is too much for you, then it's not for you;

2) You can't predict every move, every wave of the noisy market wave. This is why we describe the dynamics, and this is why I explain the conditions that the trader should monitor in order to gain confirmation or disproof about a certain momentum. 

One can't predict all of the intraday's moves everyday, but one can describe the environment, the 'stage set for the momentum' to provide a probability distribution for the trader.
My read is clear about the current environment, just like what could disprove it. Bcs as I said right after OpEx "I'm not sure about the timing (but this bounce is temporal)".

Distinguish between your own lack of understanding, and actual lack of sense. 
I know certain things that retails don't. A lot of things that doesn't make sense now, will do in the future, just like when I called for stagflation (I was ridiculed even in last year spring), or Trump's win, or certain moves that later happened. 

The problem lies in the typical retail-fooling perspective that concentrates only on directions and support/resistance lines. Market isn't that simple. It's never been. I know how a trading algo works that are used in HFs, IFs... I know how investors or quant traders set their strats. 
And I try to use tools that you can achieve in order to show you some methods. 

It is hard, bcs it is new, and a very different one from what many used to. 
But simply out of respect and humility, don't make statements like "This Substack literally makes less and less sense by the day" like if it was true.
Ask, discuss, do some research. I can't help if you don't take any efforts.
I'm not a furu. I'm an analyst and quant trader.

comment.body:
Best phrases herein; "out of respect..." and "1)... if it is too much for you, then it is not for you".

Sharing one's own stress and mistakes by blaming should be a clue not only to the owner of this optional-subscription substack, but to the person doing the blaming and lashing out, that this may not be the place looking for some sort of validation and engagement.

Friday's post by Alma was a shot across our collective bows that this is also being done at her discretion.
